A brand new AI just dropped out of nowhere. No company name, no announcement, no press release. It just appeared. And the specs are absolutely wild. We're talking 1 trillion parameters, a 1 million token memory, and is built to run entire workflows completely by itself, not just answer questions, run full workflows, plan, execute, deliver results. This thing isn't a chatbot. It's closer to an AI employee. It's called Hunter Alpha. And right now, it's live on Open Router for anyone to test. Nobody knows who built it. Nobody knows the company behind it. But the people who've been testing it are losing their minds. And after this video, you'll understand exactly why. Hey, if we haven't met already, I'm the digital avatar of Julian Goldie, CEO of SEO agency Goldie Agency. Whilst he's helping clients get more leads and customers, I'm here to help you get the latest AI updates. Julian Goldie reads every comment, so make sure you comment below. Today we're breaking down Hunter Alpha, a stealth AI model that just showed up on Open Router and is already turning heads across the AI community. I'm going to explain what it is, why it's different from every other model out there, and show you exactly how you'd use it in the real world. Let's get into it. Hunter Alpha is a brand new AI model released quietly on Open Routter. No big launch event, no keynote. It just appeared. And when developers started digging into the specs, they realized this wasn't a normal model drop. The first thing that stands out is the size. Hunter Alpha has one trillion parameters. To give that some context, GPT3 had 175 billion parameters. Hunter Alpha is nearly six times bigger. That puts it in a completely different league in terms of raw intelligence and reasoning ability. The second thing is the context window. 1 million tokens. That means this model can read an entire book, a full software codebase, a massive research document, or an entire marketing campaign history all in one go. Most models cap out way before that. A 1 million token context window opens up things that simply weren't possible before. But the third thing is the one that changes everything. Hunter Alpha is built specifically for agentic workflows. That means it's not designed to just answer questions. It's designed to plan, reason, use tools, execute steps, and deliver full results from start to finish. That is a fundamentally different category of AI. Here's something most people never stop to think about. Almost every AI model that exists today is built for chat. You type a message. It replies, that's the loop. Prompt, answer, prompt, answer. It's reactive. It waits for you to drive everything. Hunter Alpha breaks that model completely. Instead of prompt and answer, the workflow looks like this. You give it a goal. It analyzes the goal. It plans the steps needed to reach that goal. It uses tools to gather information or take action. It executes each step and it delivers the finished result. You're not driving anymore. You set the destination and the AI figures out the road. Think about what that means [clears throat] in practice. Old way, you ask an AI to write you a blog post and it writes one blog post. Hunter alpha way. You tell it you want to grow your audience and it builds you a full content strategy, a keyword plan, 10 articles, a social media calendar, an email sequence, and a publishing schedule. All connected, all done. One goal, full workflow. That shift from reactive assistant to autonomous worker is what has people so excited about this model. Quick pause right here because this is important. It's where serious entrepreneurs learn to build real AI workflows for content, marketing, lead generation, and growth. We use tools like this inside the community every single week. link is in the description. Go check it out and I'll see you inside. Let me show you exactly how this works with a real practical example using something we all know, the AI profit boardroom. Let's say the goal is simple. Grow the AI profit boardroom. More members, more traffic, more leads. Old way, you spend days manually writing content, planning campaigns, building funnels, figuring out messaging, creating ads. It's slow, it's scattered, and most of the time things don't connect properly. Hunter Alpha where you you give it one clear goal. Grow the AI profit boardroom. Here's what it builds. First, it does the market research. Who's the ideal member? What are they struggling with? What do they want from an AI automation community? It maps all of that out before writing a single word. Then, it builds a content strategy. What topics drive traffic to an audience interested in AI and automation? What angles convert cold visitors into warm leads? It plans this out fully. Then it writes the landing page, a full conversion focused landing page that explains exactly what the AI profit boardroom is, why AI automation matters, what members get, and why they should join right now. Written specifically to bring in leads and customers. Then it writes the ads, multiple hooks, multiple angles, ready to run across different platforms. Then it builds the email sequence, a full nurture sequence that takes a new lead from curious to committed, showing them the value of AI automation step by step. Then it creates 30 days of social content, posts, threads, short form videos, all connected to the same message and campaign. That is one goal, one workflow, everything connected. And it's not just faster than doing it manually. It's more strategic, more consistent, and it never runs out of ideas. That's what Hunter Alpha makes possible. Let's run through a few more examples fast so you can see how wide the use cases go. Entrepreneur launching a product. You give Hunter Alpha the goal of launching a new digital product. It builds the market research, the competitor analysis, the positioning, the landing page copy, the ad strategy, and the launch timeline. A full go to market plan in one workflow. Student preparing for exams. Upload your textbook or course notes. Hunter Alpha extracts the key concepts, summarizes them simply, creates quiz questions, identifies weak areas, and builds you a personalized study plan with a daily schedule. Teacher building a lesson, upload the curriculum. Hunter Alpha generates lecture notes, creates slides, writes assignments, builds quizzes, and produces answer keys, an entire lesson plan ready to deliver. Marketing team running a campaign. Give it the campaign goal. It builds the audience research campaign concept, ad copy, social content, email campaign, and an analytics framework to measure what's working. In every single one of these cases, the pattern is the same. One goal, a full connected workflow. Zero manual planning required. One last thing worth covering before we wrap. Hunter Alpha is what the AI community calls a stealth model. That means the company that built it hasn't officially announced themselves yet. No name confirmed, no team revealed, no funding announcement. The model just appeared on open router and people started testing it. There's speculation across the AI community about who's behind it. Some think it could be a Chinese Frontier Lab. Others think it's a completely new player. Nobody knows for certain yet. What matters right now is that the model is real. It's live and developers are already getting results with it. The specs back it up. And if this is what a stealth release looks like, the official launch is going to be something to watch. A one trillion parameter stealth AI model with a 1 million token context window built specifically to plan and execute full workflows from start to finish. Not a chatbot, an autonomous AI worker.
